blockingIssues.description: EgressFilter module exists with passing unit tests, but is NOT wired into any HTTP client path. Four HTTP clients (vault.rs, web_search.rs, opa_policy.rs, hybrid.rs) create reqwest::Client directly without applying EgressFilter middleware. The feature requires 'The filter is applied as a middleware before every outbound HTTP request made by tools or agents' — this integration is entirely missing.

git_commit function in tools.rs does NOT call SecretScanner::scan_staged() before committing. It directly runs 'git commit -m <message>' without any secret scanning. The feature explicitly requires wiring 'before git commit operations' — this wiring is absent. Commit is blocked only if the pre-commit hook is installed, but the inline scan is not performed.

Resource limit tracking is not functional: (1) record_turn() is never called so max_turns limit never triggers, (2) record_cost() is never called so max_cost_usd limit never triggers, (3) record_success() is never called so failure threshold is permanently stuck after N failures. Additionally, the circuit breaker pattern (Closed→Open→HalfOpen state transitions) is not implemented — only failure counting exists.

WriteFileTool has redundant size validation code (tools.rs:323) that runs AFTER the guardrail validate_file_size() with a different default (10 MiB vs 1 MiB). When guardrail_config.enforce_size_limit is false, the guardrail is skipped but the old check still runs with inconsistent behavior. This creates a configuration bypass where file size limits can be silently inconsistent.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on what 'wired' means for middleware integration. Workers incorrectly declared features complete when only unit tests passed without verifying actual integration. Specifically: (1) EgressFilter needs to be applied to reqwest::Client via a middleware/wrapper pattern, (2) SecretScanner needs to be called inline in git_commit before running git, (3) resource limit tracking methods (record_turn, record_cost, record_success) need to be invoked in the execution loop.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: safety-egress-filtering worker declared 'egress.rs is already fully implemented' after unit tests passed; safety-secret-scanning worker did not wire SecretScanner into git_commit; safety-resource-limits worker did not invoke record_turn/record_cost/record_success in the execution loop.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that the rust-worker skill referenced in features.json does not exist at .factory/skills/rust-worker/. Only rust, rust-patterns, rust-code-review, and rust-testing-code-review exist. Workers specified rust-worker skill should fall back to rust skill or report skill not found.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple safety features (safety-loop-detection-wiring, safety-credential-proxy, safety-resource-limits) specified skillName='rust-worker' which does not exist. Workers proceeded without noting the missing skill.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add os_sandbox.rs to the documented modules in swell-sandbox/AGENTS.md. Currently lists gvisor.rs and firecracker.rs but not os_sandbox.rs which now contains SeatbeltSandbox implementation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: swell-sandbox/AGENTS.md shows 'Key modules: lib.rs, gvisor.rs, firecracker.rs' but os_sandbox.rs with SeatbeltSandbox is now a key module.
